name: Bulgur Meatballs (Keftede)
recipeIngredient: 1 pound ground lamb, lean, see note
1/2 cup bread crumbs (finely crumbled pita bread)
1/4 cup chopped parsley, or mint or mix
3 tablespoons virgin olive oil, or less
recipeInstructions:
VARIATIONS
Use less meat.
Substitute ground turkey 1 medium egg for the large
PREPARATION (30 minutes)
- Place the bulgur in a small bowl. Add enough cold water to cover and let soak for 10 minutes. Drain through a sieve lined with cheesecloth. Wrap the cheesecloth around the bulgur and squeeze out the excess water.
OR USE INSTANT POT
- Place the chickpeas in a blender or food processor fitted with a metal blade. Blend until finely chopped. (Or coarse chop)
COOKING (30 minutes chilling and 30 minutes cooking)
- In a large mixing bowl, combine bulgur, chickpeas, lamb, bread crumbs, egg, lemon juice, garlic, coriander, cumin, salt, pepper, and mint.
- Roll the mixture into 30 balls, about 1-1/2 in (3 cm) in diameter. Place on a large plate and refrigerate for at least 20 minutes. Re-roll the meatballs if necessary to refine the shape.
- Heat half the oil in a large non-stick skillet over medium heat. Reduce the heat to low. Add half the meatballs and cook, moving and turning frequently until browned on all sides and cooked in the center, about 6 to 8 minutes. Test one for pink-center. Transfer to paper toweling when done. Cover with foil to keep warm.
- Heat the remaining oil in the skillet and cook the remaining meatballs.
- Place meatballs on a warm serving platter. Serve hot, garnished with mint and salad leaves or dandelion greens. Serve yogurt in a bowl.
